mainEntity.name: When should I stop trying to handle my neck injury claim myself and hire a lawyer?

acceptedAnswer.text: You should hire a lawyer if your claim is denied, if the insurer refuses to pay for surgery or therapy, or if they offer a settlement. Early legal advice can prevent costly mistakes.
acceptedAnswer.description:
If the insurance company is paying your bills and your check is on time, you might not need one yet. However, if they deny a test your doctor ordered, stop your checks before you're ready to work, or offer a settlement that seems low, you need a lawyer. In neck cases, many people wait until the insurance company stops paying for physical therapy—by then, valuable evidence might be lost.

Key factors include the complexity of your medical needs and the 'cooperativeness' of the insurance adjuster.

Most consultations are free. It never hurts to have a lawyer look at your case early to ensure deadlines aren't being missed in the background.

At Just Work Comp Law, we provide 'crystal-clear' communication so you know exactly where your claim stands before things go wrong.
